pages.full_text: On to David's at his new house. All the family are there. We walked through beautiful woods & went to supper at Margaret's hotel. at Chamberet.
pages.notes: Page also contains a pencil sketch of a rustic building or barn (not a route sketch). The text appears to be a continuation from the preceding page. 'Chamberet' is a commune in Corrèze, France. No handwritten page number visible on this page.

pages.full_text:
Tuesday Simon & Eve drove off at 8.45. to pick up Margaret & Helen. David & Sally left at 9 with Maia & Minor to put Minor on a train & buy a bed. Charles, Dylan & Jem slept on.... Aylen woke up & made tea for everyone.
Mid morning we left for La[?tte] des Insects at Nede. It was fascinating, with a beautiful observationants' nest, a tunnel with lit-up ant passage ways underneath. The family had just bought it in March & moved here from London.
We had lunch in the car park.
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in colour photograph/postcard of a large bee or wasp nest hanging in a tree, signed 'JM'. The place 'La[?tte] des Insects at Nede' is likely 'La Cité des Insectes' at Nedde, Haute-Vienne, France — the word between 'La' and 'des' is partially obscured but could read 'Cité' or similar; the diary spelling appears to read '[?tte]'. 'Nede' likely refers to the village of Nedde. 'observationants'' may be a compound word meaning 'observation ants'. Year cannot be confirmed from the page itself; no year annotation visible.
